slides.title: People and Work
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 6: People and Work. Welcome to Unit 6! In this unit the focus is on people and work. You will explore the many different jobs and professions that keep communities running, from physicians and lawyers to food vendors and commentators. You will learn how people choose their careers, what skills different jobs require, and how the world of work connects to ideas of fairness, justice, and community responsibility. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Identify and describe a range of professions and career paths, explaining the skills and training each one requires
Explain how personal interests and strengths can guide career choices
Read a passage about careers and jobs and answer comprehension questions at different thinking levels
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to identify and describe a range of professions and career paths, explaining the skills and training each one requires. you will also explain how personal interests and strengths can guide career choices. you will also read a passage about careers and jobs and answer comprehension questions at different thinking levels. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: wisdom, script, text, commentary, observation, caption. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes People and Work: source text 1 and People and Work: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Compound Nouns?
bullets: A compound noun is a noun made up of two or more words that come together to create a new word with a specific meaning. Understanding compound nouns is...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Compound Nouns?. A compound noun is a noun made up of two or more words that come together to create a new word with a specific meaning. Understanding compound nouns is important because they help us express complex ideas in a simple way. For example, instead of saying “the fees for tuition,” we can say tuition fees. Instead of saying “the room where the court meets,” we can say courtroom. Compound nouns make our language clearer, more precise, and more efficient. Why Do Compound Nouns Matter? Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: Job Advertisement and Career Presentation
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Career Interview Role-Play. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
